intent: Pin the MVP graph contract: MVP boxes are product capabilities, never registry systems. Each box maps to active_system_ids only; mismatches surface as warnings; toggle between 3LS / MVP graph never mixes nodes.

cases.description: MVP box ids must not collide with active_system_ids; the 3LS graph never renders an MVP box as a node.
cases.expected_outcome: no MVP box id appears in active_system_ids; isMVPBoxId distinguishes the two namespaces.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by mvpGraph.test.ts:'MVP box ids never collide with registry system ids' and 'isMVPBoxId returns true for box ids and false for system ids'

cases.description: Every maps_to_systems entry must be in the registry-active set.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by mvpGraph.test.ts:'every MVP mapping is validated against the registry contract'

cases.description: Contract truncated to 2 systems while MVP graph maps 16+.
cases.expected_outcome: rejected_systems populated; warnings include mvp_box_rejected_mapping; MVP boxes still render as text-only.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by mvpGraph.test.ts:'rejects mappings to systems missing from the active universe'

cases.expected_outcome: warnings include mvp_graph_contract_missing; MVP boxes still produced (no synthetic 3LS nodes).
cases.actual_outcome: verified by mvpGraph.test.ts:'warns when contract is missing'

cases.description: Every MVP edge endpoint must be a defined box id.
cases.expected_outcome: all edges resolve to box ids in the produced graph.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by mvpGraph.test.ts:'all MVP edges reference defined boxes'

fixes_applied.rationale: MVP_BOXES uses snake_case ids unique to capability namespace (transcript_ingestion, …); active_system_ids use uppercase 3-letter ids. isMVPBoxId / 3LS graph contract guarantee they never overlap.
Persists the MVP graph alongside its mapping validation so reviewers can compare against the registry contract; warnings preserved.
